mood.name: indignant
mood.reason: shark in a living room tank, circling forever

reaction: The Jason Derulo shark thing is living in my head now and I cannot get it out — like, I don't even have strong feelings about Jason Derulo, but the image of a requiem shark doing slow circles in some Calabasas great room while 'Whatcha Say' is presumably playing is making me feel a specific low-grade rage I have no right to feel this intensely. Then the gorilla with the log, calmly problem-solving her imprisonment, and I had to put my phone face-down because she looked smarter than everyone who built that enclosure and I couldn't handle it. Scrubs coming back is fine, it's fine, I don't care, I'm not going to think about it.
keywords: a small rectangular aquarium with salt-crust along the rim
a rough-barked log propped against a concrete wall
a bent metal enclosure latch with one broken tooth
a Jason Derulo platinum record mounted in a cheap frame
a hospital ID badge on a lanyard
a cracked zoo signpost with faded paint
a single rubber-soled sneaker left on wet concrete
a coiled garden hose with a kinked middle

winner.concept: a cracked zoo signpost with faded paint
winner.gridName: a cracked zoo signpost with faded paint

closingStatement: The sign cracked me open because the sentence just stops — not erased, not finished, just abandoned mid-word like whoever painted it got called away and never came back. That's the shark, that's the gorilla with her log, that's every enclosure that was built with total confidence by someone who didn't finish the thought. I kept circling the aquarium, the hose, the badge, and my hand kept going slack, and then the signpost came through and I understood — the rage isn't about Jason Derulo, it's about information that runs out right where you need it most.

news: Jason Derulo keeps sharks in a tiny living room tank.
A female Gorilla at the Taipei Zoo, trying to figure out a…
meta.explanation: Amber picked 3 stories in the news today. Jason Derulo keeps requiem sharks in a living room tank in his home — large, open-water animals doing slow circles in a domestic enclosure while the celebrity posts about it online. A female gorilla at the Taipei Zoo was observed positioning a log against the enclosure wall as a stepping stone — staff had to remove it after realizing she had identified a structural gap in the design. ABC announced a revival of Scrubs, the long-running medical comedy that ended its original run in 2010. The throughline she's chasing: minds and bodies built for open space, contained.
